title: 中考六选五 15: Choosing a Role Model Wisely
passage:
Many teenagers have role models. They may admire athletes, singers, businesspeople, teachers or even classmates. 1. ______ A good role model can give students direction, but a poor choice can lead them to value the wrong things.

Some students admire only success itself. They see fame, money or awards and want the same results. 2. ______ If they ignore the effort behind success, they may develop unrealistic expectations.

A better way is to look at the qualities behind achievement. Does the person work hard? Do they treat others responsibly? Do they keep improving after failure? 3. ______ These questions help students learn from the person, not just worship the person.

Students should also remember that no role model is perfect. A person may be excellent in one area but weak in another. 4. ______ Blind admiration can stop students from thinking clearly.

Finally, students can have more than one role model. They may learn discipline from an athlete, creativity from an artist and kindness from a teacher. 5. ______ In this way, role models become sources of useful qualities rather than objects of blind following.

questions.label: Questions 1-5 - Sentence Insertion
questions.instruction: Choose the best sentence from A-F for each blank in the passage. There is one extra sentence you do not need.

choices.text: However, the person we choose to admire matters.
But they may not notice the years of practice, failure and self-control.
This can help them build a richer and more balanced idea of growth.
So students should admire strengths while still keeping judgment.
The real value of a role model is not only what they have, but how they became better.
Students should copy everything their role models do.
